focus: DX audit — dead extracted modules, duplicated patterns, type safety, lint warnings

findings.description: Module created in round 1-2 to deduplicate chat message deserialization but NEVER imported anywhere. The original inline code remains in src/ai/chat.ts:482-492, src/api/ai.ts:410-418, and src/ui/api/ai/ai-sessions.ts:109-117. Either wire up the import or delete the file.
findings.fix: Replace inline loops in chat.ts, api/ai.ts, ai-sessions.ts with `import { deserializeChatMessages } from './message-utils.js'`

findings.description: Module created in round 1-2 exporting ACTION_VERBS and LIST_PREFIX but NEVER imported. Duplicates remain in src/ai/tools/builtin/extract-tasks-from-text.ts:223-227 (as local vars) and src/ui/components/ExtractTasksModal.tsx:42-46 (as local consts).
findings.fix: Import ACTION_VERBS and LIST_PREFIX from task-extraction-patterns.ts in both files, delete the local copies

findings.description: Chat message deserialization (filter system msgs, parse toolCalls JSON, cast role) duplicated in 3 locations. The extracted helper in message-utils.ts already does this correctly with error handling.
findings.fix: Use deserializeChatMessages() from message-utils.ts in all 3 call sites

findings.description: Magic string "http://localhost:1234/v1" repeated 8 times across 3 files for the default LM Studio base URL. Should be a single constant in config/defaults.ts.
findings.fix: Add DEFAULT_LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:1234/v1' to config/defaults.ts and import everywhere

findings.description: Magic strings "http://localhost:11434" and "http://localhost:11434/v1" for default Ollama URL repeated in 2 places.
findings.fix: Add DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL to config/defaults.ts

findings.description: Residual console.log duplicates the logger.info on line 123. The logger already outputs structured JSON to stdout.
findings.fix: Remove line 124

findings.description: 3 `as any` casts for Web Speech API: window.SpeechRecognition (line 32), recognition.maxAlternatives (line 45), event params (lines 52, 61). A web-speech.d.ts ambient declaration would eliminate all of them.
findings.fix: Create src/ai/voice/adapters/web-speech.d.ts declaring SpeechRecognition, SpeechRecognitionEvent, SpeechRecognitionErrorEvent interfaces

findings.description: 4 `as any` casts (lines 53, 73, 103, 131). Lines 103/131 cast FileSystemDirectoryHandle to access `.keys()` iterator — this is a known DOM type gap. Lines 53/73 cast voice IDs for the piper-tts-web library.
findings.fix: Add an ambient type declaration for FileSystemDirectoryHandle.keys() and type the voice ID parameter more precisely

findings.description: `DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS.includes(color as any)` — the `as const` tuple doesn't accept `string` in `.includes()`. Fix with a typed helper.
findings.fix: Use `(DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS as readonly string[]).includes(color)` instead of casting color

findings.description: `baseUrlOverride as string` — unnecessary cast since baseUrlOverride is already typed via destructuring on line 132/160. If it could be undefined, the `||` fallback handles it without a cast.
findings.fix: Remove the `as string` cast; let the `||` chain handle the undefined case naturally

findings.description: `plugin.instance as any` to access .store on the timeblocking plugin. Should use a typed interface for plugin instances that expose a store.
findings.fix: Define a TimeblockingPluginInstance interface with { store: TimeblockingStore } and cast to that instead of any

findings.description: `toLocalModelInfo(provider: any, ...)` uses duck typing instead of a proper interface. An interface like `LocalModelProvider` with status, preload, etc. would eliminate the any and the runtime checks.
findings.fix: Define a LocalModelProvider interface in the voice types and use it instead of any

findings.description: `toolCalls?: any` in the inline type for restoredMessages. Should use the ToolCall type from ai/types.ts.
findings.fix: Import ToolCall type and use `toolCalls?: ToolCall[]`

findings.description: `progress_callback: (event: any) => { ... }` — the HuggingFace transformers library has a typed ProgressCallback. Even a minimal `{ status: string; progress?: number }` interface would be better.
findings.fix: Define a ProgressEvent interface { status: string; progress?: number } and use it

findings.description: React Hook useEffect missing dependency 'nav'. Could cause stale closure bugs when nav reference changes.
findings.fix: Add nav to the dependency array or extract nav.setMode/nav.setDate as stable refs

findings.description: React Hook useEffect missing dependency 'model'. The effect debounces model list fetching but won't re-fetch when model changes.
findings.fix: Add model to the dependency array

findings.description: useMemo has unnecessary dependency 'modelVersion'. If modelVersion is just a counter to force re-computation, document it with a comment; otherwise remove it.
findings.fix: Add a comment explaining modelVersion is intentional for cache-busting, or restructure

findings.description: Unused `eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` directive — the line it guards no longer triggers the rule.
findings.fix: Remove the eslint-disable comment on line 18

findings.description:
4 remaining eslint-disable + any casts for plugin component types. A generic `PluginComponent

>` type would eliminate all of them.
findings.fix: Use the existing PluginComponent type from plugins/ui-registry.ts more broadly, or parameterize it

findings.description: 20+ `args.xxx as string` casts throughout the file. The tool args come from JSON Schema-validated input but TypeScript sees them as unknown. A typed args parser per tool would be cleaner.
findings.fix: Define per-tool arg interfaces and validate/cast once at the top of each handler

findings.description: `// TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context` has been present since early development. The routing is handled elsewhere (server.ts, cli/index.ts). This TODO is misleading.
findings.fix: Remove the TODO or replace with a comment explaining the entry point architecture

findings.description: Inline deserialization does not wrap JSON.parse(row.toolCalls) in try-catch (line 489), unlike the extracted message-utils.ts which does. Corrupted toolCalls JSON would crash session restore.
findings.fix: Use the shared deserializeChatMessages() which already handles this safely

findings.description: Same issue as DX-R3-22: JSON.parse(row.toolCalls) unguarded in the API route handler for session switching.
findings.fix: Use the shared deserializeChatMessages() helper

findings.description: `Readable.fromWeb(res.body as any)` — Node.js types don't perfectly align ReadableStream<Uint8Array> from fetch with the web ReadableStream type. This is a known Node.js typing issue and the cast is justified.
findings.fix: No action needed — document with a comment explaining the Node.js web-stream type gap

findings.description: ESLint reports 73 warnings total: ~45 are @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any (mostly in plugin/voice/AI code), ~15 are react-refresh/only-export-components (cosmetic), 3 are react-hooks/exhaustive-deps (potential bugs), 1 is an unused eslint-disable. No errors.

summary.critical_issues: 2 dead extracted modules (message-utils.ts, task-extraction-patterns.ts) created in rounds 1-2 but never wired up — the original duplicated code remains
summary.type_safety: ~10 reducible `as any` / `as string` casts across voice adapters, plugin system, and AI tools
summary.duplicated_patterns: Chat message deserialization repeated 3x, LM Studio base URL repeated 8x, Ollama URL repeated 2x
summary.lint_health: 73 warnings (0 errors) — 3 are potential bugs (missing hook deps), rest are cosmetic
